title: I Trained With The World's Best Surfers

chunks.title: Introduction to Training
chunks.summary: The video introduces the setting in Malibu where the speaker plans to undertake pool workouts with expert surfer Luca Padawa. The speaker shares his limited experience in surfing and breath holding, setting the stage for a day filled with challenging activities.
chunks.transcript: You take a steak and you put the steak in the blender. [Music] What's up, guys? So, I made it to Malibu. I'm here at Lar Hamilton, Gabby Reese's house. This pool is legendary. And I'm here with my buddy Luca Padawa, one of the most decorated, most incredible big wave surface in the world. There is no hyperbole in that statement, guys. Luca's going to put me through a pool workout. Gabby and Lear are gonna give me some wisdom and put me through a pool workout. I'm gonna get to hang out in the sauna with these guys. Like, this is amazing. So, I'm gonna take you guys with me on this day. Uh, you guys might know that I'm a surfer, but I basically suck at surfing, so I'm just intermediate at best. Uh, I have a pool in Costa Rica. I love breath holding, but I'm a novice of this, too. So, you're going to get to see me suffer in this video, and uh hopefully Luca doesn't have to give me CPR or, you know, any sort of uh rescue breathing. But, thank you for taking me to do this,

chunks.title: Pool Training Techniques
chunks.summary: Luca discusses the complexities of pool training, highlighting the stress of breath control while submerged. They focus on developing skills like CO2 tolerance and mental fortitude through a mix of breath-holding durations and vertical jumps, simulating the conditions faced in big wave surfing.

chunks.title: Surfboard Insights
chunks.summary: Luca explains the characteristics of an unconventional surfboard designed for towing into giant waves, emphasizing its weight and balance for navigating rough water. The discussion also covers the different types of surfboards used in various surfing conditions.

chunks.title: Mental Focus in Surfing
chunks.summary: The conversation delves into the mental state required when surfing massive waves, including the focus necessary to manage fear and execute maneuvers successfully. They also reflect on the nature of memories from high-stress situations within the surf environment.
chunks.transcript: had some good moments, but there's a great line that goes, "Big waves aren't measured in feet and inches. They're measured in increments of fear." >> So, you're talking about a fourdimensional, fivedimensional force, right? >> How do we just put a two-dimensional measurement on? You can't like think about, yes, height is one thing, but also the volume, the power. Leard's got a great line. He says waves are like dogs. You could have a small dog that'll bite the [ __ ] out of you, and you could have a big fluffy dog that's super friendly, >> right? And you could have a big giant dog that'll bite the [ __ ] out of you too. So waves are the same way. There's certain waves that >> are a lot shorter in that two-dimensional often like obsessed over measurement that are >> a lot more scary than some of the giant ones that you see. >> We're dudes. All we think about is [ __ ] length and size. And it's like, you know, I'm not a big wave surfer. So I'm just thinking like, and I think most people watching this are like all they think about. I mean, I'm a intermediate surfer at best. And people ask me like, "What's the biggest wave you ever surfed?" Sometimes you're in like you were just saying with like podcasting and sharing information on health and diet like sometimes you're just in the flow and sometimes you're not. And it just depends. you're kind of in this intermittent back and forth between being, okay, I need to really focus right now because this is some rugged terrain and I need to make sure I get right there with a certain amount of speed and if I don't do that, this whole thing is going to come crashing down on me. And there's sometimes where it's a little bit more it's free and it's fun and even if it's in a big wave, you're in less of a survival mode. chunks.title: Nutrition for Big Wave Surfing
chunks.summary: In the concluding segment, the speakers highlight the importance of proper nutrition for endurance during long surf sessions. They share recipes for nutritious smoothies that can sustain energy levels and performance throughout the day.
